title: Eugenia standleyi
taxonomy: Eugenia standleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. The Myrtaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 6,000 species found worldwide. Eugenia standleyi is a member of the genus Eugenia, which includes approximately 1,500 species of flowering plants. The genus Eugenia is classified under the order Myrtales, a group of plants characterized by their showy flowers and aromatic foliage. Eugenia standleyi is native to the tropical regions of Central America and is often found in humid, subtropical forests.
uses: Eugenia standleyi is a plant with reported medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues, including fever and pain. The plant's extracts are also used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. In addition, Eugenia standleyi is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. More research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

The plant is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. The plant has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its use in traditional medicine. Eugenia standleyi has also been found to exhibit antidiabetic and hepatoprotective effects in preclinical studies, suggesting its potential benefits for managing diabetes and liver health.
